stem: A photographer keeps an archive copy of their best work on a Blu-ray disc.
model_answer: Optical storage (such as CDs, DVDs and Blu-ray discs) represents data as microscopic pits and lands on the surface of a reflective disc. A laser beam is shone at the surface and reflections are read back to detect 1s and 0s. Optical discs are cheap, portable and non-volatile, but are slower than SSDs and can be scratched easily.

mark_points.text: data is represented by pits and lands on the surface of a disc
accepted_alternatives: bumps and flats on the disc

mark_points.text: a laser is shone at the disc and reflections are detected to read 1s and 0s
accepted_alternatives: laser light reads the pattern back
